h1: Give Your Horse 100% Hoof Protection, Comfort, and Safety:
text: Give Your Horse 100% Hoof Protection, Comfort, and Safety: GET 15% OFF ANY SIZE CAVALLO BOOTS NOW: Yes - I Want Boots Now As a horse owner, are you fed up with tender soles, lost shoes, damaged hooves, bacterial infections, laminitis, navicular, or just tired of nailing metal shoes? Better Hoof Health Backed By Science: Western Kentucky University Research Recommends Cavallo Trek Hoof Boots for Hoof Protection, Traction, and Reduced Pressure: Dr. Jennifer Gill and Gabriella Lynn of the Western Kentucky University Equine Science Department completed the first independent university study comparing hoof boots to traditional metal shoes. They chose their preferred boots for the study – the Cavallo Trek. Results confirmed that Cavallo Hoof Boots benefit horses, provide comfort, protection and minimize potential hoof damage. The study focused on surface area, force, and pressure exerted on horses’ hooves while travelling on the hard surface of asphalt. The Scientists used FujiFilm imaging to determine the amount of pressure felt in the horse’s hooves, which allowed the scientists to see dynamic pressure through colour mapping. From Dr. Jennifer Gill: “We found that the Cavallo Trek Hoof Boots were significantly lower in pressure than the other two treatments,” Gill said. “And this is what we expected from our data. title: Cavallo Hoof Boots Return Policy
text: Exchange & Return Policy Arranging an Exchange All returns/exchanges must be arranged with the original place of purchase. For products purchased directly from Cavallo Horse & Rider (www.cavallo.local), please contact us by phone or email to arrange your return/exchange. ALL exchanges/returns must first be PRE-AUTHORIZED and a return authorization number must be obtained. IMPORTANT – When Arranging a Sizing Exchange, Please Note the Following: Call Cavallo toll-free at 1-877-818-0037 or email krystle@cavallo.local). Provide your original order number and the quantity, style, colour and size of the boots you will be exchanging. Please indicate why an exchange is needed (i.e. boots are too large, too small, etc.) and indicate what size you will be needing or if you need our help finding your replacement size(s). Cavallo does not cover costs for return shipping but does cover the cost of shipping any new items to the customer in exchange. When sending your returned item(s) back to Cavallo, please take care to insure your parcel against damage and/or loss and obtain a receipt with the tracking number. Cavallo can not accept responsibility for returned packages that are lost or damaged in transit. All Returned Items Must be in As-New Condition: Boots sent back without first arranging an exchange and obtaining a return authorization number will be returned at the sender’s expense. Th

title: Cavallo Horse Hoof Boots Free Laminitis Guide Download
h1: A Free Laminitis Guide to Help You & Your Equine
text: A Free Laminitis Guide to Help You & Your Equine It's a Gift We Hope You Never Need... Are you going through a Laminitis event with your horse, or just wanting to be prepared in case it happens in the future? Healing from Laminitis can be a tough journey for a horse, but in some ways, it can be even tougher on its owner. This guide is something you can tuck away in your toolkit, in case you ever need it. Your Guide to Using Cavallo Boots for Laminitis Recovery will “behoove” both you and your equine. Here’s everything you need to know about using Cavallo Boots to assist in Laminitis recovery or any hoof rehabilitation situation. It’s important to know that you are not alone. So many share these feelings of hopelessness and fear when guiding their beloved horse through the healing process. But know that there truly can be light at the end of the tunnel. Read real stories from your fellow horse owners who’ve been through it all and come out the other side. It’s simple. And it works. You’ll see!

title: Cavallo Pastern Wraps
h1: Cavallo Pastern Wraps for Additional Pastern Protection
text: Cavallo Pastern Wraps for Additional Pastern Protection Cavallo Pastern Wraps enhance comfort and protection for your horse’s pasterns and heel bulbs, ideal for full-time hoof boot wear or sensitive skin. Essential for conditions like laminitis, founder, and navicular disease where your horse will be wearing the boots full-time. Cavallo Pastern Wraps reduce friction, ensuring a smooth transition to booted life during break-in and extended wear. Table of Contents When to Use Cavallo Pastern Wraps Properly Size and Fit Cavallo Pastern Wraps Putting on Pastern Wraps Cavallo Testimonial s Other Pastern Protection Options Why Choose Cavallo Hoof Boots When to use Cavallo Pastern Wraps? Cavallo Pastern Wraps are ideal when your horse needs extra protection, comfort, or cushioning around the pastern and heel bulbs. Here are the key times and situations to use them! Riding and High-Intensity Work for horses engaged in high-intensity activities, Pastern Wraps add protection against rubbing. The thin neoprene reduces friction and shields sensitive areas (such as your horse's heel bulbs and pastern) during repeated movements or exposure to rough terrain. Full-Time Boot Wear Horses that need to wear Hoof Boots full-time should always use Pastern Protection. These are horses managing chronic conditions like laminitis, founder, navicular, or thin soles. title: The Science Behind Cavallo Hoof Boots - Western Kentucky University
text: The Science and Research Behind Cavallo Hoof Boots Western Kentucky University Research Recommends Cavallo Trek Hoof Boots for Hoof Protection, Traction, and Reduced Pressure. First Independent University Hoof Boot Study Dr. Jennifer Gill and Gabriella Lynn of the Western Kentucky University Equine Science Department have just completed the first independent university study comparing hoof boots to traditional metal shoes. They chose their preferred boots, Cavallos, for this study. Results confirmed what we in the horse community have been witnessing since 2006. Cavallo Trek Hoof Boots benefit horses by providing comfort, protection, and minimizing potential hoof damage. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wY7DYZTNSPs Specialized Film Measures Hoof Pressure The study focused on surface area, force, and pressure exerted on a horse’s hooves while travelling on the hard surface of asphalt. The Scientists used FujiFilm imaging to determine the amount of pressure felt in the horse’s hooves. This film procedure allowed the scientists to see dynamic pressure through colour mapping. First Independent University Hoof Boot Study “The Cavallo Hoof Boots protected the entire hoof itself, which we thought was very beneficial, especially over some other types of hoof protection, such as horseshoes,” Dr. Gill said. “We also found that the tread of the Cavallo Trek Hoof Boots was thicker and would
